pi_items.source: Task description (line 5) and task.json hooks (lines 13-14) both specify this filename

Geometric calculation: cross-section area (π×3²=28.274 mm²) × path length with 20mm radius fillets at B and C (294.635 mm) = 8330.6 mm³. Verified via Python geometric analysis of path coordinates A(0,0,0)→B(0,100,0)→C(100,100,50)→D(100,0,50) with fillet adjustments.
This is a tolerance/constraint parameter used by verifier.py (line 22) to define acceptable volume range. Reasonable tolerance for 3D modeling with fillets.

Geometric calculation: Path Y extent is 0 to 100 mm (100 mm range). Wire diameter is 6 mm (3 mm radius on each side). Bounding box = 100 + 2×3 = 106 mm.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified and accurate. The task requires creating a bent wire in FreeCAD with a path through coordinates A(0,0,0)→B(0,100,0)→C(100,100,50)→D(100,0,50), 20mm radius fillets at corners B and C, a circular 6mm diameter profile, saved as 'seat_frame_wire.FCStd'. Expected volume is 8330.6 mm³ (calculated from 28.27 mm² cross-section × 294.6 mm adjusted path length). Bounding box dimensions are exactly 106×106×56 mm, accounting for the 3mm wire radius extending beyond the geometric path in all directions.
